label: no
cot_prefix: Okay, let's go through this step by step. The question is about fitting a working telephone booth. The options are A, B, C, D. First, the story mentions that the man from the telephone department got off the bus and went to the tea stall. Then, it says: "It was a year ago that the phone line had been installed, six months later men from the public works department had come to put up the phone booth." So the phone line was installed a year ago, then six months later the booth was put up. That would make the total time since the line was installed as 1.5 years. But the question is about fitting a working telephone booth. The phone booth was installed six months after the line, but then it was painted, and there's a mention of a painter taking a day to paint the sign. However, the man says later that the minister will come next month to inaugurate it. So the booth was installed but not yet working because it needed inauguration. But the question is about the process of fitting the booth. The phone line was a year ago, the booth six months later. So the process of fitting the booth took six months after the line? Or the entire process from line to booth was 18 months? Wait, the question is about fitting a working telephone booth. The phone line was installed a year ago, and the booth was put up six months after that. But the booth wasn't working yet because the minister has to inaugurate it. But the actual installation of the booth (the structure) was done six months after the line. So the process of putting up the booth was done in six months after the line. But the question is asking which option fits the process of fitting a working booth. Let's check the options. Option A: was a process that had already taken 12 months. The phone line was installed a year ago, but the booth was put up six months after that. So the booth installation itself wasn't 12 months, but the line was 12 months. So A is incorrect. Option B: was in the charge of the headman. The headman is mentioned later, but the installation was done by the public works department. The headman received the telephone number chit. So B is incorrect. Option C: should have been finished more than a year ago. The phone line was a year ago, but the booth was installed six months after the line. So if the entire process (line and booth) was supposed to take less time, maybe it should have been done earlier. But the story doesn't say it should have been finished more than a year ago. The line was a year ago, the booth six months later. The total time from line to booth was six
